# Mixture Weibull GH model quantities

Evaluates the survival function (`"S"`), cumulative hazard (`"H"`), or
hazard (`"h"`) of a two-component mixture Weibull GH model.

## Usage

``` r
mixWeib(res = "S", t, p, lambda1, lambda2, gamma1, gamma2, X, beta1, beta2)
```

## Arguments

- res:

  Character: `"S"`, `"H"`, or `"h"`.

- t:

  Numeric vector of time points.

- p:

  Mixture proportion (weight of the first component), in (0, 1).

- lambda1, lambda2:

  Scale parameters of the two Weibull components.

- gamma1, gamma2:

  Shape parameters of the two Weibull components.

- X:

  Numeric covariate value (scalar or vector).

- beta1:

  AFT-type covariate coefficient.

- beta2:

  PH-type covariate coefficient.

## Value

Numeric vector of the requested quantity.

## Examples

``` r
t <- seq(0.01, 5, length.out = 100)
mixWeib("h", t, p = 0.8, lambda1 = 0.1, lambda2 = 0.1,
        gamma1 = 3, gamma2 = 1.6, X = 0, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5)
#>   [1] 0.002042962 0.006810689 0.011477747 0.016906152 0.023292221 0.030723090
#>   [7] 0.039247867 0.048898049 0.059695399 0.071655520 0.084789635 0.099105529
```
